meta.title: Extract Audio from Video — Free, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Extract the audio track from any video and save it as MP3, right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Extract Audio from Video — Free, No Upload
og.description: Save the soundtrack of any video as an .mp3, locally in your browser.
hero.title: Extract audio from a video — in your browser
hero.lead: Drop a video and save its audio track as an .mp3 — the music, the speech, the soundtrack. It's pulled out on your own device, so the file is never uploaded.
prose.h2: Pull the sound out of any video
prose.p1: Whether it's a song from a music video, the dialogue from a clip, the audio of a recorded call or the soundtrack of a film, extracting the audio gives you a small file you can listen to anywhere — no video to carry around, no app to install. The result is a standard MP3 that plays on every device.
prose.p2: Most tools that do this upload your video to a server first, which is slow and means trusting them with your footage. {{SITE_NAME}} extracts the audio locally in your browser instead, so nothing is stored or logged, there's no size limit, and your video never leaves your device.
faq.a1: No. The extraction happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — open your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.q2: What format is the extracted audio?
faq.a2: MP3, at a bitrate you choose (128–320 kbps). It's the most widely compatible audio format, so the file plays in any music app, podcast player or car stereo.
faq.a3: Most common ones — MP4, MOV, MKV, WebM and AVI among them. The audio track is decoded and written out as a standard .mp3 entirely on your device.
